channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Putin presides over BRICS summit seeking to expand Russia's clout
item.description: KAZAN, Russia (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Thursday presided at the closing session of a summit of the BRICS bloc of developing economies, praising its role as a counterbalance to what he called the West's “perverse methods.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike on Gaza shelter kills 17 as Blinken says cease-fire talks will resume
item.description: Palestinian officials say an Israeli strike on a school where displaced people were sheltering in the central Gaza Strip has killed at least 17 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korean president raises possibility of supplying Ukraine with arms
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s president on Thursday raised the possibility of supplying Ukraine with weapons while stressing that his government “won’t sit idle” as North Korea allegedly sends troops to support Russia’s aggression toward its neighbor.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trash carried by a North Korean balloon again falls on the presidential compound in Seoul
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Trash carried by a North Korean balloon fell on the presidential compound in central Seoul on Thursday for the second time, raising concerns about the vulnerability of key South Korean sites.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Turkey strikes Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day
item.description: Turkey has struck suspected Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day after a deadly attack on the premises of a key defense company.
item.dc:creator: Suzan Fraser, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Indonesia says its coast guard drove away Chinese ship that interrupted survey in disputed sea
item.description: Indonesian patrol ships drove a Chinese coast guard vessel away from a survey vessel in a disputed area of the South China Sea for the second time in three days, Indonesian authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Edna Tarigan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man pleads guilty to displaying a Nazi symbol at pro-Palestinian rally
item.description: SYDNEY (AP) — A restaurateur pleaded guilty Thursday to displaying a Nazi symbol during a pro-Palestinian rally in Sydney in early October. Alan Yazbek was photographed at the Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French foreign minister says conference for Lebanon raised $1 billion in pledges
item.description: France’s foreign minister says Paris' international conference for Lebanon raised $1 billion in pledges for humanitarian aid and military support.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tropical storm battering Philippines leaves at least 24 people dead in flooding and landslides
item.description: Widespread flooding and landslides set off by a tropical storm in the northeastern Philippines left at least 24 people dead, swept away cars and prompted authorities to scramble for motorboats to rescue trapped villagers, some on roofs.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US confirms North Korea has sent 3,000 troops to Russia for training and possible Ukraine combat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. said Wednesday that 3,000 North Korean troops have been deployed to Russia and are training at several locations, calling the move very serious and warning that those forces will be “fair game” if they go into combat in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, LOLITA C. BALDOR and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel accuses 6 Al Jazeera journalists in Gaza of being Palestinian militants
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The Israeli army on Wednesday accused six Al Jazeera journalists covering the war in Gaza of also being current or former paid fighters for Palestinian militant groups. Al Jazeera rejected the claims.
item.dc:creator: JACK JEFFERY and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Liberal lawmakers ask Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau not to run for a fourth term
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Some lawmakers in Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's own Liberal Party asked the leader not to run for a fourth term Wednesday, handing him one of the biggest tests of his political career.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Kristy strengthens into a Category 4 storm in the Pacific Ocean
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Hurricane Kristy strengthened into a Category 4 storm in the Pacific Ocean on Wednesday and was expected to remain away from land as it churns in the Pacific Ocean, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican troops kill 19 suspected cartel members, suffer no casualties: officials
item.description: Mexican army troops have reportedly killed 19 suspected cartel affiliates during a Monday shootout near Culiacan, the state capital of Sinaloa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli weapon seen in rare AP photos of Beirut airstrike appears to be a powerful smart bomb
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — In all but the blink of an eye, an Associated Press photographer's camera captured the moments that a battleship-gray Israeli bomb plummeted toward a Beirut building before detonating to bring the tower down.
item.dc:creator: ADAM SCHRECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's population has fallen by 10 million since Russia's invasion, UN says
item.description: The United Nations said Ukraine's population has decreased by around a quarter, or 10 million people, since Russia's invasion of the country in February 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Situation in Haiti worsens amid ‘acute violence’ as UN support mission falters under gang violence
item.description: The gang violence in Haiti has gotten worse despite the deployment of U.N.-sanctioned international police officers, forcing mass displacement, hunger among millions and even famine for thousands of Haitians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Belarus sets a Jan. 26 election that's almost certain to extend its authoritarian leader's rule
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Lawmakers in Belarus on Wednesday set the next presidential election for Jan. 26, a vote almost certain to extend the three-decade rule of authoritarian leader Alexander Lukashenko, who has suppressed all political dissent.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indigenous Australian Senator Lidia Thorpe intensifies criticism of King Charles III
item.description: CANBERRA, Australia (AP) — An Indigenous senator has intensified her criticism of King Charles III, again accusing the British monarch of complicity in the “genocide” against Australia’s First Nations peoples and declaring on Wednesday she will not be “shut down.” Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walmart employee found dead inside walk-in oven at Canada store: police
item.description: Authorities in Canada are continuing an investigation into the death of a Walmart employee who was found inside a walk-in oven at the store.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken urges Netanyahu to seek truces in Lebanon, Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday to discuss ending the conflict in Lebanon and Gaza in addition to securing pathways for humanitarian aid, according to a readout from the meeting.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Iran Revolutionary Guard officer charged in plot to kill American author in US
item.description: The Justice Department unsealed an indictment against four Iranian officials Tuesday for their alleged involvement in a murder-for-hire targeting Iranian-American journalist Masih Alinejad.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: Police said they haven't yet confirmed the cause and manner of death of the woman, who worked at the store, nor has her name been released.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, NewsNation

item.title: Woman wedged between boulders in Australia for hours
item.description: The woman had apparently dropped her phone and was trying to retrieve it while hiking in Australia, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy says a US green light on a NATO invitation will sway Germany
item.description: Ukraine's president is hoping allies will take a more positive stance on his “victory plan” after the U.S. election.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Binational delegation heads to Mexico City to talk sewage, border waits, stalled port of entry
item.description: The delegation is led by the San Diego Regional Chamber of Commerce, which says their mission is to "champion U.S.-Mexico relations and advance issues critical to the success of the binational business community as the newly sworn-in Mexico Administration begins its session.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Blinken heads to Middle East in wake of Sinwar's killing
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is going to the Middle East in the wake of the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Voting from abroad: What to know
item.description: Most U.S. citizens who reside outside the country can vote absentee in primary and general elections.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea demands withdrawal of North Korean troops allegedly helping Russia fight Ukraine
item.description: South Korea issued a demand to Russia to condemn the cooperation between Russia and North Korea, calling for the withdrawal of North Korean troops from Russia's war with Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats urge Biden to push Israel for press freedom in Gaza
item.description: Dozens of House Democrats are pressing the Biden administration to get tougher on Israel when it comes to press freedoms in Gaza, warning that a lack of media access in the war-torn territory has stifled any push for accountability surrounding military operations that have killed tens of thousands o
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Sanders: Trump will be 'closer to Netanyahu' on Gaza if reelected
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) said Sunday he believes former President Trump will have similar views of the Israel-Hamas war to those of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu if he is reelected.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: NHC monitoring Hurricane Oscar as it nears Cuba
item.description: Hurricane Oscar is being closely monitored by the National Hurricane Center as it makes its way toward Cuba on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Some electricity was restored in Cuba, the government said Saturday, after the island nation's worst blackout in at least two years left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Japan's governing party headquarters is attacked with firebombs and suspect is arrested
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s governing party in Tokyo on Saturday, then crashed his car into the fencing of the prime minister's residence, police said. There were no reports of injuries.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Funeral home in Poland apologizes after a corpse falls out of a hearse in traffic
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — A funeral home in Poland issued an unusual apology on Saturday after a corpse that it was transporting fell out of a hearse and into traffic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Japan’s ruling party HQ attacked with firebombs; man arrested: Reports
item.description: A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s ruling party in Tokyo Saturday, media outlets report.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama/The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah vows a new phase in the war, as Israel says it has killed Hamas leader Sinwar
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Lebanese militant group Hezbollah vowed to launch a new phase of fighting against invading Israeli troops Friday, a day after Israel said its forces in the Palestinian enclave of Gaza had killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, the chief architect of last year’s brutal attack on Israe
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9
